summary: American Realist painter from Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania. Known for Christina's World (paralyzed girl in pink dress crawling toward Olson House in Cushing, Maine), the Helga Pictures (247 portraits of neighbor Helga Testorf), and Winter 1946. Son of illustrator N.C. Wyeth, father of Jamie Wyeth.

works.name: Christina's World (1948)
works.description: Paralyzed woman in pink dress crawling through grass toward the Olson House in Cushing, Maine. Christina Olson had Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease (often cited as polio). Farnsworth Art Museum operates the house. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Paralyzed/polio-crippled woman in pink dress crawling in grass toward a farmhouse (Olson House) in Cushing, Maine
examples: a woman in a pink dress crawls in the direction of the Olson House
The Farnsworth Art Museum operates a wooden house in Cushing, Maine that appears in the background

clues.clue: Maga's Daughter: wife Betsy modeled in place of Christina Olson (Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease)
examples: The painting Maga's Daughter depicts this artist's wife Betsy, who modeled in place of a woman with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease

clues.clue: Inspired Bernard Langlais's wooden sculpture Local Girl
examples: A painting by this artist inspired Bernard Langlais's wooden sculpture of a woman with logs for arms titled Local Girl

works.name: Helga Pictures (1971-1985)
works.description: 247 portraits of German neighbor Helga Testorf from Chadds Ford, many nude, kept secret from wife Betsy. Purchased by Leonard Andrews. Braids, Lovers, Overflow, Black Velvet, The Prussian are individual works.

clues.clue: 247+ portraits of German immigrant neighbor Helga Testorf, many nude; kept secret from wife Betsy; purchased by Leonard Andrews
examples: This artist created 247 portraits of a German immigrant from Chadd's Ford with long blonde braids named Helga Testorf
Time ran a cover story on this artist's series of paintings of his neighbor Helga Testorf

clues.clue: Lovers: nude blonde-braided woman on a stool by open window, illuminated by beams of light
examples: A leaf blows through an open window illuminating a naked woman on a stool
A nude with blonde braids sits next to an open window in this artist's Lovers

clues.clue: Braids: solemn woman in gray turtleneck wearing her hair in braids
examples: a solemn woman in a gray turtleneck wears her hair in the titular Braids

clues.clue: Best-selling catalog; first art catalog selected by Book-of-the-Month Club; VHS narrated by Charlton Heston
examples: first ever art catalog selected by the Book-of-the-Month Club

clues.clue: Helga originally worked as nurse for Karl Kuerner; Michael Palin first to interview her
examples: Former Monty Python member Michael Palin was the first to interview the subject of these works, who originally worked as a nurse for Karl Kuerner

works.name: Winter 1946
works.description: Boy in earflaps/oversized military wear running down Kuerner's Hill after the artist's father N.C. Wyeth was killed in a train accident at that spot.

clues.clue: Boy running down Kuerner's Hill; first painting since illustrator father was killed in train/car accident at that site
examples: A boy in earflaps runs down Kuerner's Hill in this artist's Winter 1946, the first painting since his illustrator father was killed
This artist's work entered a melancholy period after a train accident killed his father

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Son of N.C. Wyeth (illustrator of Treasure Island for Scribner's). Father of Jamie Wyeth (Portrait of Pig, pumpkinhead self-portrait, JFK portrait). Egg-tempera technique from brother-in-law Peter Hurd. Based in Chadds Ford, PA and Cushing, ME. Brandywine Valley. Other works: Snow Hill, The Master Bedroom, Barracoon, Breakup, Chambered Nautilus.

clues.clue: Son of N.C. Wyeth (illustrator of Treasure Island for Scribner's); father of Jamie Wyeth (Portrait of Pig, JFK portrait)
examples: This artist of Winter 1946 and The Master Bedroom was the father of a painter who did a controversial posthumous portrait of John F. Kennedy; he was also the son of an artist who illustrated Treasure Island for Scribner's Classics

clues.clue: Based in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania; Brandywine Valley scenes
examples: This artist, who often depicted his birthplace of Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania

clues.clue: Egg-tempera technique from brother-in-law Peter Hurd
examples: This artist's brother-in-law Peter Hurd taught him the egg-tempera technique

clues.clue: Barracoon: depicted a naked Black woman; actually painted white model (Helga) as Black
examples: In one painting, this artist depicted a white model as black and claimed the model was his family's maid Betty Hammond

clues.clue: Breakup: painting of his own severed hands on a block of ice
examples: Bronze casts of this artist's hands were recently auctioned along with the painting they inspired; that painting depicts the hands emerging from an ice floe

comprehensive_summary:
Andrew Wyeth was an American Realist painter based in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania and Cushing, Maine, the son of illustrator N.C. Wyeth and father of painter Jamie Wyeth.

Christina's World (1948) is his most famous painting, depicting a paralyzed woman (Christina Olson, who had Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease) in a pink dress crawling through grass toward the Olson House in Cushing, Maine. The Farnsworth Art Museum operates the house.

The Helga Pictures comprise 247+ portraits of his German neighbor Helga Testorf, many nude, created secretly over 14 years in Chadds Ford. They were purchased by Leonard Andrews and became a sensation when revealed in cover stories by Time and Newsweek. Individual works include Lovers (nude woman on stool by window), Braids, Overflow, Black Velvet, and The Prussian.

Winter 1946 depicts a boy running down Kuerner's Hill, the first painting after his father was killed in a car/train accident at that site. He learned egg-tempera from brother-in-law Peter Hurd. Other works include Snow Hill, The Master Bedroom, Barracoon (painted Helga as a Black woman), Breakup (hands on ice), and Chambered Nautilus.
